top_classes.name: Sample No. 353, The Hunt and Salisbury Collection at the U.S.Geological Survey, Denver, Co.
Sample No. 358, The Hunt and Salisbury Collection at the U.S.Geological Survey, Denver, Co.

This somewhat heterogeneous sample consists of bands of pure fine-grained to microcrystalline dolomite, alternating with bands of coarser dolomite crystals and subangular aligned quartz grains showing pressure solution texture. There is also a greenish-brown material (clay) scattered throughout the sample in small amounts. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.sedimentary.limestone.fine.limest2.spectrum.txt

Ooids in the sample are small (0.5-0.75 mm) with laminar structures largely destroyed by dolomitic recrystallization. Carbonate crystals show a heterogeneous distribution, from microcrystalline to fine medium-grained. Abundant quartz fragments and some altered plagioclase (albite twinning), opaques, carbonaceous material, ferruginous clay, and a brown-green to colorless micaceous mineral are also present. Particle size was 0 - 75 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.sedimentary.limestone.fine.limest7.spectrum.txt
Meerdink et al. 2019, Baldridge et al. 2009
